summary: Recent updates in the Firefox browser's connection transport area enhance its handling of real-time communications. Notably, improvements were made to safely read STUN (Session Traversal Utilities for NAT) messages, ensuring that data is accurately processed even if it's improperly aligned in memory. Additionally, new statistics related to RTCCertificates—used for secure connections in WebRTC (the technology behind video calls)—were introduced, including details on certificate IDs, which help in managing security. Furthermore, the implementation of candidate-pair packet statistics allows for better monitoring of network performance during calls by tracking packets sent and received, ultimately contributing to a more reliable user experience in real-time communications. **This emphasis on security and performance boosts the reliability of web-based video calls and streaming, which is essential in today's digital interactions.**
